document: The Capital Markets (Corporate Governance) (Market Intermediaries) Regulations-1

sections.heading: Transitional provision ............................................................................................................................................................................... 11
sections.title: One-year transition period for licensed intermediaries
sections.summary: Market intermediaries licensed before these regulations commenced must comply with the regulations within one year of the regulations' commencement. This ensures a smooth transition without immediate disruption.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides a transition period for data transfers and processing compliance, relevant for historical data handling.

requirements.text: Market intermediary licensed before regulations commenced must comply within one year of regulations' commencement

sections.source_quote: A market intermediary that was licensed before the commencement of these Regulations shall comply with these Regulations within one year of the commencement of these Regulations.

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.title: Prohibition on using market intermediary facilities for personal gain
sections.summary: Directors, management, and staff must not use the market intermediary's name or facilities for personal advantage in political, investment, or retail transactions. They also cannot use their connection to borrow from or become indebted to clients. Additionally, they are prohibited from using their position to obtain preferential treatment in purchasing goods, shares, or securities. This prohibition may lead to dismissal and prosecution.
sections.dtia_summary: Prohibits misuse of market intermediary facilities for personal gain, which could impact cross-border data transfers if client financial data is involved in such transactions.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff must not use the market intermediary's name or facilities for personal advantage in political, investment or retail purchasing transactions
Directors, management and staff must not use their connection with the market intermediary to borrow from or become indebted to clients
Directors, management and staff must not use their position to obtain preferential treatment in purchasing goods, shares and other securities

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff must not use the market intermediary's name or facilities for personal advantage in political, investment or retail purchasing transactions, or in similar types of activities.

sections.heading: Directors
sections.title: Prohibition on insider trading and misuse of confidential information
sections.summary: Directors, management, and staff are prohibited from trading securities when they possess non-public information that could materially affect the market price. They also cannot influence others to trade or share such information, which is considered insider dealing and is a crime. This section applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: Prohibits insider trading and misuse of confidential information, which could affect data transfers involving client financial information across borders.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff should not deal in securities when in possession of non-public information that could materially affect market prices
Directors, management and staff prohibited from influencing others to deal in securities or communicating insider information

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff should not deal in the securities of any company listed or pending listing on a stock exchange at any time when in possession of information... which would likely bring a material change in the market price of the shares or other securities of the company concerned.

sections.heading: Fit and proper requirements for appointment as director
sections.title: Requirement for accurate and complete financial records
sections.summary: Accounting records and reports must be complete and accurate, with no false entries that obscure transaction nature or mislead authorization limits. All records and computer files must be used only for their intended management purposes to ensure integrity and prevent fraud.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures accurate financial records, which is critical for data transfers involving financial information to prevent misrepresentation or fraud.

requirements.text: Accounting records and reports must be complete and accurate
No false entries or entries that obscure the true nature of transactions or mislead authorization limits
All records and computer files must be accessed and used only for management purposes for which they were originally intended

sections.source_quote: Accounting records and reports must be complete and accurate. Directors, management and staff should never make entries or allow entries to be made for any account, record or document of the market intermediary that are false and would obscure the true nature of the transaction...

sections.heading: Register of directors
sections.title: Confidentiality of client information and transactions
sections.summary: Directors, management, and staff must protect client information and transactions from disclosure, except in the proper course of duty or with written consent. They cannot use client information for financial gain. Business information may be shared with third parties only with client consent or as required by law.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires strict confidentiality of client data, which is essential for data transfers to prevent unauthorized access or breaches.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff must take precautions to protect client information and transactions
No staff, management or director may disclose or use secrets, copyright material, or client correspondence without written consent or proper duty
No staff, management or director may use information obtained for financial gain
Business and financial information about clients may be shared with third parties only with prior written consent or as required by law

sections.source_quote: Confidentiality of relations and dealings between the market intermediary and its clients is paramount... No member of staff, management or director should during, or upon and after termination of employment... divulge or make use of any secrets, copyright material, or any correspondence...

sections.heading: The Board
sections.title: Fair and equitable treatment of clients and staff
sections.summary: All business dealings with clients, staff, and those relying on the market intermediary must be conducted fairly and equitably without influence from friendship or association. Decisions must be arms-length business decisions, avoiding preferential transactions with insiders unless fully documented and authorized by the Board.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures fair treatment in transactions, which is critical for data transfers to prevent bias or preferential handling of client data.

requirements.text: All business dealings with clients, staff, and those relying on the market intermediary must be conducted fairly and equitably
Staff, management and directors must not be influenced by friendship or association in meeting client requirements or recommendations
Decisions must be made on a strictly arms-length business basis
Preferential transactions with insiders must be avoided or, if transacted, must be fully documented and authorized by the Board

sections.source_quote: All business dealing on behalf of the market intermediary with the current potential clients, with other members of staff and with those who may have cause to rely upon the market intermediary should be conducted fairly and equitably.

sections.heading: Strategic direction and control
sections.title: Prohibition on insider loans and credit facilities
sections.summary: Market intermediaries in Kenya cannot grant unsecured advances to employees or their associates. Loans to officers, significant shareholders, or their associates must be fully secured, approved by the Board, and on terms similar to ordinary clients. Any loans must not be made in a fraudulent or reckless manner.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricts insider loans, which is critical for data transfers involving financial information to prevent conflicts of interest.

requirements.text: Market intermediary shall not grant or permit unsecured advances to employees or their associates
Market intermediary shall not grant unsecured or non-fully secured advances to officers, significant shareholders, or their associates
Loans to directors or other management participants must be approved by the full board of directors
Loans must be made in the normal course of business on terms similar to ordinary clients
Market intermediary must notify the Authority within seven days of approval for loans to directors
Loans must not be made in a fraudulent or reckless manner or otherwise than in compliance with the Act and regulations

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff should not use their positions to further their personal interests. A market intermediary shall not in Kenya therefore—

sections.heading: Code of conduct
sections.title: Code of conduct for market intermediary staff
sections.summary: The board may adopt or develop a code of conduct for directors, management and staff that addresses issues specified in the Schedule, but the Schedule's code of conduct takes precedence if the developed code omits issues or is inconsistent with it. This applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires market intermediaries to establish codes of conduct that may impact data handling practices and employee data management during data transfers.

requirements.text: The Board may adopt the code of conduct set out in the Schedule or develop a code of conduct for directors, management and staff
Where the code of conduct developed by a market intermediary does not address all issues or is inconsistent with the Schedule, the Schedule shall apply to the extent of omission or inconsistency

sections.source_quote: The Board may adopt the code of conduct set out in the Schedule or develop a code of conduct for the directors, management and staff that addresses the issues specified in the code of conduct set out in the Schedule: Provided that where the code of conduct developed by a market intermediary does not address all the issues specified or is inconsistent with the code of conduct set out in the Schedule, the code of conduct set out in the Schedule shall apply to the extent of the omission or inconsistency.

sections.heading: Board charter
sections.title: Board charter requirements for market intermediaries
sections.summary: The board must prepare a charter confirming responsibility for strategic plans, operational performance monitoring, policy determination for risk management and internal controls, delegation of powers, conflict of interest management, and identification of key risk areas requiring regular monitoring. This applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates board charters that impact data transfer impact assessments by requiring specific risk management and internal control frameworks for financial data handling.

requirements.text: Confirm responsibility for adoption of strategic plans, monitoring operational performance, and determination of policy for risk management and internal controls
Reserve specific powers for itself and delegate other matters to management
Provide a corporate code of conduct addressing conflict of interest for directors and management, regularly reviewed and updated
Identify key risk areas requiring regular monitoring

sections.source_quote: (1) In order to discharge its responsibilities, the board shall prepare and write a charter that— (a) confirms its responsibility for the adoption of strategic plans, monitoring the operational performance, the determination of policy and processes that ensure that the integrity of the market intermediary's risk management and internal controls; (b) reserves specific powers to itself and delegates other matters to the management of a market intermediary; (c) provides a corporate code of conduct that addresses conflict of interest, relating to directors and management, which shall be regularly reviewed and updated as necessary; and (d) identifies key risk areas, that require regular monitoring.

sections.heading: Accountability and responsibility
sections.title: Board accountability for market intermediaries
sections.summary: The board must be responsible for the performance and conduct of the market intermediary's business, maintain a schedule of matters reserved for its decision, and not be discharged from duties for matters delegated to committees or management. This applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes board accountability that impacts data transfer impact assessments by requiring oversight of data handling practices and decision-making processes.

requirements.text: Be responsible and accountable for the performance and conduct of the market intermediary's business
Keep and maintain a schedule of matters reserved for its decision
Direct and control matters delegated to committees or management

sections.source_quote: The board shall be responsible and accountable for the performance and conduct of the business of the market intermediary. The board shall keep and maintain a schedule of the matters reserved for its decision and ensure that it directs and controls. The board shall not be discharged from its duties and responsibilities for matters or authority delegated to committees of the board or to the management of a market intermediary.

sections.heading: Board meetings
sections.title: Board meeting frequency for market intermediaries
sections.summary: The board must meet at least once every three calendar months to review market intermediaries' processes, procedures, and internal control effectiveness, and prepare an annual schedule of meetings at the beginning of each financial year. This applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires regular board meetings that impact data transfer impact assessments by ensuring periodic reviews of data handling and control mechanisms.

requirements.text: Meet at least once in every three calendar months to review market intermediary's processes and procedures and effectiveness of internal systems of control
Prepare an annual schedule of meetings at the beginning of each financial year

sections.source_quote: The board shall meet at least once in every three calendar months to review the market intermediary's processes and procedures and the effectiveness of its internal systems of control. The board shall, at the beginning of each financial year, prepare an annual schedule of the meetings of the market intermediary.

sections.heading: Remuneration of directors
sections.title: Director remuneration standards for market intermediaries
sections.summary: The remuneration of directors and chief executives must be commensurate with the market intermediary's operations and the remuneration offered for similar positions in the market. This applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: This section sets remuneration standards that may indirectly impact data transfer impact assessments through governance structures affecting data handling practices.

requirements.text: Remuneration of directors and chief executive must be commensurate with the nature and size of operations and remuneration for similar positions in the market

sections.source_quote: The remuneration of directors and the chief executive of a market intermediary shall be commensurate with the nature and size of operations of the market intermediary and the remuneration offered for similar positions in the market.

sections.heading: Committees
sections.title: Board committees and reporting procedures
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must establish an audit committee and other committees with written terms of reference, reporting procedures, and authority scope. The audit committee reviews internal audit reports, financial statements, risk management policies, internal controls, external auditors' work, compliance with codes of conduct, and conducts special investigations. Committee decisions require board ratification to be binding.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires internal governance structures that impact data handling through committee oversight of financial and operational controls, which may affect data processing and storage practices across borders.

requirements.text: Board shall establish audit committee and other committees as necessary
Committees must specify terms of reference, reporting procedures, and authority scope in writing
Audit committee must review internal audit reports for management and management's response
Audit committee must review periodical financial statements and financial reports
Audit committee must review audited and unaudited financial statements before public release
Audit committee must review effectiveness of risk management policy
Audit committee must review internal controls affecting financial performance and reporting
Audit committee must review external auditors' proposed audit scope and approach
Audit committee must monitor compliance with code of conduct and ethics
Audit committee must consider compliance work plan
Audit committee must regularly report to board on activities, issues, and recommendations
Audit committee must institute and oversee special investigations when necessary
Board may refer matters to committees for consideration and determination
Committee decisions do not bind market intermediary unless presented to board for ratification

sections.source_quote: 13. Committees (1) The board shall establish an audit committee and such other committees, as it considers necessary and specify their terms of reference, in writing, including the reporting procedures and a written scope of authority.

sections.heading: Corporate governance framework
sections.title: Corporate governance framework requirements
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must establish a corporate governance framework providing strategic guidance for board accountability and timely documentation of financial, ownership, and governance information. Boards must review management, operations, accounts, capital expenditure, and corporate performance quarterly, and review governance structures annually, documenting review outcomes.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates robust governance frameworks that influence data handling practices, particularly through quarterly financial reviews and documentation requirements affecting data storage and processing.

requirements.text: Market intermediary shall establish corporate governance framework providing strategic guidance for board accountability
Corporate governance framework must ensure timely and accurate documentation of financial structure, performance, ownership, and governance
Board shall review management, operations, accounts, major capital expenditure, and corporate performance quarterly
Board shall review corporate governance structure annually
Board shall document results of reviews conducted quarterly and annually

sections.source_quote: 14. Corporate governance framework (1) A market intermediary shall establish a corporate governance framework that provides— (a) strategic guidance of the market intermediary that promotes the effective monitoring of the management and accountability of the board; and (b) for availability and documentation of timely and accurate information relating to the market intermediary, including its financial structure, performance, ownership and governance.

sections.heading: Responsibilities of shareholders
sections.title: Shareholder responsibilities and oversight
sections.summary: Shareholders must jointly and severally protect and exercise authority over market intermediaries in general meetings. They must elect qualified directors, ensure board accountability, and change board composition when necessary. Shareholders must also approve acquisitions or transfers resulting in control over five percent or more of shares, and market intermediaries cannot appoint shareholders with over 25% shareholding as executives.
sections.dtia_summary: Shareholder oversight mechanisms impact data governance through board accountability and restrictions on executive appointments, which may affect data processing and storage practices.

requirements.text: Shareholders shall jointly and severally protect and exercise authority over market intermediary in general meetings
Shareholders must elect or appoint persons with relevant experience and qualifications to board of directors
Shareholders must ensure board is held accountable through general meetings
Shareholders must use powers to change board composition when directors fail to perform
Shareholders must apply for Authority approval for acquisitions or transfers resulting in control over five percent or more of shares
Market intermediary must obtain approvals before share allotment
Market intermediary cannot appoint shareholders with more than twenty-five percent shareholding as executive directors or senior management

sections.source_quote: 15. Responsibilities of shareholders (1) The shareholders of a market intermediary shall, jointly and severally, protect, preserve and actively exercise the authority over the institution in general meetings.

sections.heading: Appointment of employees
sections.title: Employee appointment policies
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must establish and obtain approval from the Authority for employee appointment policies. The board must review these policies at least every three years and submit any changes to the Authority for approval.
sections.dtia_summary: Employee appointment policies influence data handling through governance requirements that affect data processing and storage practices.

requirements.text: Board shall formulate employee appointment policy approved by Authority
Board shall review employee appointment policy at least every three years
Board shall submit policy changes to Authority for approval

sections.source_quote: 16. Appointment of employees (1) The board shall formulate a policy for the appointment of employees, which shall be approved the Authority.

sections.heading: Chief executive officer
sections.title: Chief executive officer responsibilities
sections.summary: The chief executive officer must implement board policies, recommend competent officers, coordinate departments, establish internal controls, design management information systems, appraise the board on operations, and ensure compliance with laws. Market intermediaries cannot change shareholders, directors, CEOs, or key personnel without Authority approval.
sections.dtia_summary: CEO responsibilities impact data handling through internal control systems and compliance requirements, which may affect data processing and storage practices.

requirements.text: CEO must implement board policies and corporate strategy
CEO must identify and recommend competent officers for market intermediary operations
CEO must coordinate operations of departments within market intermediary
CEO must establish and maintain efficient internal control systems
CEO must design and implement management information systems
CEO must regularly appraise board on market intermediary operations
CEO must ensure market intermediary complies with Act and other relevant laws
Market intermediary cannot change shareholders, directors, CEOs, or key personnel without Authority approval

sections.source_quote: 17. Chief executive officer (1) The chief executive officer of a market intermediary shall be responsible to the board for the day to day running of the market intermediary and shall— (a) implement the policies and the corporate strategy developed by the board; ...

sections.heading: Separation of employees' duties
sections.title: Employee duty separation
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must maintain adequate separation of employee duties, particularly between those responsible for incurring commitments, making payments, and preparing accounts. They must also implement internal controls, functional lines, and systems to restrict information flow between key departments.
sections.dtia_summary: Duty separation requirements impact data handling through internal control mechanisms that affect data processing and storage practices.

requirements.text: Market intermediary must maintain adequate separation of employee duties between commitment incurring, payment making, and account preparation
Market intermediary must maintain internal controls, functional lines, and systems to restrict information flow between key departments

sections.source_quote: 18. Separation of employees' duties (1) The management of a market intermediary shall maintain adequate separation of employee duties, particularly between— (a) those responsible for incurring commitment; (b) those responsible for making payments; and (c) those responsible for preparing accounts.

sections.heading: Employees
sections.title: Employee fitness and proper qualifications for market intermediaries
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must ensure all employees possess necessary qualifications, experience, and integrity. The board must verify employees' fitness for roles, including no evidence of lack of integrity or issues affecting their financial management. Management must document specific qualifications for each position and meet regulatory requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires employee vetting and documentation to ensure staff are fit for roles, relevant to internal controls and risk management in financial operations.

requirements.text: Board ensures all employees are fit and proper for roles with necessary qualifications and experience
Management determines and documents experience and qualifications for each post
Management ensures all employees have documented training program based on needs and regulatory requirements

sections.source_quote: The board shall ensure that all employees are fit and proper for their roles, including having the necessary qualifications and experience for their responsibilities and that there is no evidence of lack of integrity or other matters likely to raise concerns over their probity and capacity in managing their own financial affairs or those of clients.

sections.heading: Management of a market intermediary
sections.title: Market intermediary management responsibilities
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must operate daily, implement board policies, adhere to operational systems, follow planning processes, promote HR development, comply with conduct codes, and maintain records. Management must ensure employees have job descriptions, understand conduct codes, possess necessary knowledge, and have defined areas of discretion with oversight.
sections.dtia_summary: Sets operational and compliance standards for market intermediaries, ensuring internal controls and oversight mechanisms are in place for financial risk management.

requirements.text: Management operates and manages market intermediary on day-to-day basis
Management implements and adheres to board-developed policies, practices and standards
Management adheres to systems for efficient operations and communications
Management adheres to planning processes for achieving targets and objectives
Management promotes human resource development and staff issues
Management complies with code of conduct, the Act and relevant laws
Management keeps and maintains records and complies with reporting requirements
Each employee has a job description defining duties and responsibilities
All employees familiarize themselves with and adhere to the code of conduct
Employees collectively have necessary knowledge, skills, information and authority to establish, operate and monitor internal controls
Areas of discretion and action criteria for employees are adequately defined with oversight by another employee
Employees' ability to commit market intermediary to expenditure or trading matters is sufficiently defined
There are adequate financial controls including dual signatures for material payments

sections.source_quote: The management of a market intermediary shall— (a) implement and adhere to the policies, practices and standards developed by the board; (b) adhere to the systems established to facilitate efficient operations and communications; (c) adhere to the planning process that has been developed to facilitate achievement of targets and objectives; (d) promote human resource development and training and deal with other issues relating to staff; (e) comply with the code of conduct, the Act and any other relevant laws; and (f) keep and maintain record, and comply with all the reporting requirements.

sections.heading: Finance officers and internal auditors
sections.title: Finance officers and internal auditors must be ICPAK members
sections.summary: The chief finance officer and internal audit function responsible persons must be members of the Institute of Certified Public Accountants of Kenya (ICPAK). This ensures financial and audit personnel meet professional standards.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires finance and internal audit staff to hold specific professional qualifications relevant to financial controls and risk management.

requirements.text: Chief finance officer or internal audit function responsible person must be ICPAK member

sections.source_quote: The chief finance officer or any other person who is responsible for the finance department of a market intermediary and the person responsible for the internal audit function, shall be required to be members of the Institute of Certified Public Accountants of Kenya (ICPAK).

sections.heading: Internal audit.
sections.title: Internal audit function establishment and charter
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must establish an effective internal audit function. The board must create an internal audit charter that defines the purpose, authority, and responsibilities of the function, ensuring it evaluates and improves risk management, control, and governance processes.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires a formal internal audit function to assess and improve risk management and control systems, critical for financial oversight.

requirements.text: Market intermediary establishes an effective internal audit function
Board formulates internal audit charter to evaluate and improve risk management, control and governance processes
Internal audit charter provides assurance on management processes identifying significant risks
Internal audit charter confirms effective operation of internal control system
Internal audit charter provides credible processes for risk management and assurance feedback
Internal audit charter ensures board receives reliable information from management

sections.source_quote: The market intermediary shall establish an effective internal audit function. The board shall formulate an internal audit charter to bring a systematic, disciplined approach to evaluate and improve the effectiveness of risk management, control and governance processes and define the purpose, authority and responsibility of the internal audit function.

sections.heading: Internal auditor
sections.title: Internal auditor appointment and responsibilities
sections.summary: The board must appoint an internal auditor who is not the compliance officer, has sufficient authority, direct access to the board, and develops an internal audit program under audit committee oversight. The auditor must submit quarterly reports to the committee.
sections.dtia_summary: Specifies internal auditor qualifications and reporting requirements to ensure independent oversight of financial and operational controls.

requirements.text: Internal auditor not be the compliance officer and not involved in audited functions
Internal auditor has sufficient authority to carry out functions
Internal auditor has direct access to the board
Internal auditor develops internal audit program under audit committee oversight
Internal auditor submits quarterly reports to audit committee

sections.source_quote: The board shall appoint an internal auditor who shall— (a) not be the compliance officer and shall not be involved in any function that is being audited; (b) have sufficient authority to carry out his function as an internal auditor; (c) have direct access to the board; (d) subject to the oversight of the audit committee, develop an internal audit programme; and (e) submit quarterly reports to the audit committee.

sections.heading: Responsibility for risk management
sections.title: Risk management responsibilities
sections.summary: The board must develop and implement risk assessment and management processes, regularly review effectiveness. Management must be accountable for designing, implementing, monitoring and integrating risk management into daily operations. The process must address compliance, payment systems, operational, human resource, technology, business continuity, credit, market, reputational, political and other risks. The board and management must document policies, communicate to employees, maintain contingency plans, and evaluate them regularly.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires comprehensive risk management frameworks addressing financial and operational risks, critical for data security and regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Board develops and implements risk assessment and management process, regularly reviews effectiveness
Management accountable to board for designing, implementing, monitoring and integrating risk management into daily operations
Risk assessment process addresses compliance, payment systems, operational, human resource, technology, business continuity, credit, market, reputational, political and other risks
Board and management develop documented risk management policies and processes
Management communicates risk management policies to all employees
Management maintains backup and contingency plans for information technology failure, record loss and business premises loss
Management makes arrangements for business continuity in case of key personnel loss
Management evaluates contingency plans on a regular basis

Board appoints risk management officer to assist in risk management, review integrity of risk control systems, define risk management function, monitor external developments, provide independent oversight and review information presented by management

sections.source_quote: The board shall be responsible for the development and implementation of the process of risk assessment and management and shall regularly review the effectiveness of the process.

sections.heading: Information management system
sections.title: Board's information management system requirements
sections.summary: The board must develop an information management system that tracks implementation of policies, risk realization, market positions, and financial status of market intermediaries. This system must provide real-time insights into the intermediary's operational health and risk exposure. The requirement applies to all market intermediaries operating under this regulation.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates a board-level information system that monitors financial and risk data, requiring market intermediaries to maintain robust internal reporting mechanisms for cross-border data flows and financial transparency.

requirements.text: Board develops and implements an information management system
System provides information on policy implementation
System tracks risk realization
System monitors substantial market positions
System reflects financial position of market intermediary

sections.source_quote: The board shall develop and implement an information management system that provides information relating to its implementation, the effect of the board's policies and procedures, the realisation of risks, substantial market positions and the financial position of the market intermediary.

sections.heading: Responsibility for internal controls
sections.title: Board's internal control responsibilities
sections.summary: The board must establish and monitor internal control systems that ensure effective operation. This includes developing policies, verifying system functionality, and maintaining procedure manuals for internal controls. The requirement applies to market intermediaries to ensure their internal controls meet regulatory standards.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires market intermediaries to maintain robust internal controls that impact data handling practices, particularly for financial data and risk management across borders.

requirements.text: Board is responsible for market intermediary's internal control system
Board establishes and monitors internal control policies
Board verifies system effectiveness

sections.source_quote: The board shall be responsible for the market intermediary's system of internal controls. The board shall establish and monitor appropriate policies on internal controls and satisfy itself that the system is functioning effectively.

sections.heading: Role of management and employees
sections.title: Management and employee roles in internal controls
sections.summary: Market intermediary management must implement board policies on risk and internal controls. Employees must participate in internal control activities to ensure objectives are met. This section emphasizes accountability across all levels of the organization for maintaining effective internal controls.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates clear roles for management and employees in internal controls, which affects how data is handled and protected within market intermediaries.

requirements.text: Management implements board policies on risk and internal controls
Management identifies and evaluates risks for board consideration
Management designs, operates and monitors internal control system
Employees are responsible for internal control as part of their accountability

sections.source_quote: The management of a market intermediary shall implement the board's policies on risk and internal controls. The employees shall be responsible for internal control as part of their accountability towards the achievement of the objectives of the market intermediary.

sections.heading: Periodical review of internal controls
sections.title: Periodic internal control reviews
sections.summary: Management must report internal control monitoring activities to the board. The board must periodically review internal control effectiveness based on management's reports and document findings and actions taken. This ensures ongoing oversight of internal controls.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires regular internal control reviews that impact data handling practices, particularly for financial data and risk management across borders.

requirements.text: Management reports internal control monitoring activities to board
Board periodically reviews internal control effectiveness
Board documents results and conclusions of reviews

sections.source_quote: The board shall periodically review and enquire, based on the information and assurances provided to it by management of a market intermediary, to determine the effectiveness of internal controls established by the management of a market intermediary.

sections.heading: Compliance officer
sections.title: Compliance officer responsibilities
sections.summary: The board must appoint a compliance officer with specific powers to monitor regulatory compliance, access information, report non-compliance, and ensure accountability. The officer may be personally liable for non-compliance with regulatory requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates a compliance officer role that ensures regulatory adherence, which impacts data handling practices and cross-border data transfers for market intermediaries.

requirements.text: Board appoints compliance officer who monitors regulatory compliance
Compliance officer has sufficient authority to carry out functions
Compliance officer has unfettered access to information
Compliance officer has direct access to board
Compliance officer takes action to rectify non-compliance
Compliance officer reports non-compliance issues to board
Compliance officer reports material regulatory breaches to board
Compliance officer submits annual corporate governance report to board
Officer in charge of compliance may be personally liable for non-compliance

sections.source_quote: The board shall appoint a compliance officer who shall— (a) monitor compliance with the regulatory requirements prescribed by the Authority, and shall not be involved with any function that is the subject of compliance; (b) have sufficient authority to carry out such function; (c) have unfettered access to information; (d) have direct access to the board; (e) take necessary action to rectify any non-compliance; (f) report any non-compliance issues that cannot be rectified to the board; (g) report to the board any material breaches of the regulatory requirements; and (h) submit an annual corporate governance report to the board.

sections.heading: Receipt of client funds
sections.title: Client funds receipt procedures
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must implement systems to deposit client funds directly into client bank accounts, avoiding cash handling by employees. This ensures proper financial segregation and reduces fraud risks.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates direct client fund deposits, which impacts data handling practices for financial transactions and cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Market intermediary establishes systems for direct client fund deposits
All client funds must be deposited in intermediary's client bank account
Employees must not handle client cash

sections.source_quote: A market intermediary shall establish and implement systems that ensure that all funds received on behalf of clients are deposited directly in the intermediary's client bank account to ensure employees avoid the receipt of cash.

sections.heading: Employee records
sections.title: Employee records must demonstrate qualification and fitness assessments
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must maintain records proving each employee's qualifications, experience, fitness, and propriety. These records include job applications with verification, job descriptions, qualifications, remuneration details, securities transactions with permission, conflict of interest declarations, and holdings of publicly traded securities. The records must be kept to demonstrate effective assessment of employee suitability.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed employee records to ensure compliance with qualification and fitness standards before data transfers or processing involving employees.

requirements.text: Market intermediary shall keep records demonstrating effective assessment of employee qualifications, experience, fitness and propriety
Employee records must include job application with copy of documentation verifying qualifications and experience
Employee records must include job description
Employee records must include qualifications, experience and training
Employee records must include remuneration details
Employee records must include securities transactions with details of permission received
Employee records must include declarations of existing or potential conflicts of interest
Employee records must include details of all publicly traded securities owned

sections.source_quote: A market intermediary shall keep and maintain records relating to each of its employees demonstrating that it has effectively assessed all relevant qualification, experience, fitness and propriety.

sections.heading: Third party records
sections.title: Third party records for contracted services
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must maintain records for third parties they contract with, including the contract details, third party's legal status and verification documents, and qualifications of employees engaged. The records must ensure the market intermediary remains responsible for the delegated functions.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires third-party records to ensure accountability in data transfers and processing with external entities, critical for cross-border data transfers involving third parties.

requirements.text: Market intermediary shall maintain appropriate records for third parties undertaking functions on its behalf
Records must include the contract specifying services to be provided
Records must include details of third party's legal status, verification documents and financial viability documentation
Records must include qualifications and experience of employees to be engaged
Delegation of functions does not discharge market intermediary from responsibility for proper execution of delegated functions

sections.source_quote: Where the market intermediary contracts with a third party to undertake any functions on its behalf, it shall maintain appropriate records, including—

sections.heading: Exemption or variation of applicability
sections.title: Exemptions and variations from regulatory requirements
sections.summary: The Authority may exempt or vary the application of specific regulations for market intermediaries if it considers it appropriate in special circumstances. Market intermediaries must provide reasons for seeking exemptions and alternative arrangements. They must comply with regulations until the Authority formally exempts them, and the Authority must publish exemptions and variations.
sections.dtia_summary: Allows for regulatory exemptions but requires ongoing compliance and reporting, relevant for data transfers where specific conditions are met.

requirements.text: Authority may exempt or vary application of regulations 13, 15(5), 18, 21, 22, 23, 24(6) and 30
Exemptions must specify the period for which they are valid
Market intermediary must provide reasons for seeking exemption or variation
Market intermediary must specify alternative arrangements to comply with regulatory requirements
Market intermediary must comply with regulations until Authority formally exempts it
Authority must give written reasons for granting exemption or variation
Authority must publish exemption or variation
Market intermediary must report changes in circumstances relevant to exemption or variation
Authority may revoke exemption or variation if circumstances change

sections.source_quote: The Authority may, upon an application made in writing by a market intermediary, where it considers it appropriate in the special circumstances of the market intermediary, exempt from or vary the application of regulations 13, l5(5), 18, 21, 22, 23, 24(6) and 30 to the market intermediary

sections.heading: Remedial measures and administrative sanctions
sections.title: Remedial measures for unfit directors or officers
sections.summary: Market intermediaries must terminate services of directors or officers found unfit and proper, implement mitigation mechanisms for client or business losses, and inform the Authority immediately. Directors are jointly liable for losses from contraventions, and non-compliance with regulations constitutes a disciplinary offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires immediate action for unfit personnel, critical for data transfer integrity and risk mitigation in financial services.

requirements.text: Market intermediary must terminate services of director or officer found unfit and proper
Market intermediary must implement mechanisms to mitigate losses from termination
Market intermediary must inform Authority immediately of termination actions
Directors are jointly liable to indemnify market intermediary for losses from contraventions
Market intermediary contravening regulations commits a disciplinary offence

sections.source_quote: When a director or an officer is assessed and found not to be fit and proper to work for a market intermediary, the affected market intermediary shall: (a) be required to terminate the services of such a director or officer

sections.heading: Transitional provision
sections.title: One-year transition period for licensed intermediaries
sections.summary: Market intermediaries licensed before these regulations commenced must comply with the regulations within one year of the regulations' commencement. This ensures a smooth transition without immediate disruption.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides a transition period for data transfers and processing compliance, relevant for historical data handling.

requirements.text: Market intermediary licensed before regulations commenced must comply within one year of regulations' commencement

sections.source_quote: A market intermediary that was licensed before the commencement of these Regulations shall comply with these Regulations within one year of the commencement of these Regulations.

sections.heading: Conflict of Interest
sections.title: Conflict of interest disclosures for employees
sections.summary: Employees must disclose outside business activities that conflict with market intermediary interests, including financial interests in clients, part-time employment, corporate directorships, and trusteeships. Approval from management or board is required before engaging in such activities.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires conflict of interest disclosures to ensure data transfers and processing are not compromised by employee conflicts.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff must disclose business activities that compete or conflict with market intermediary's interests
Financial interest in client must be disclosed immediately to management and client
Affected director must not be involved in client dealings while financial interest exists
Business activities outside market intermediary during office hours constitute conflict of interest
Part-time employment must be discussed with manager and approved by board or chief executive
Employees must not solicit corporate directorships without board approval
Employees may act as directors of non-profit public service corporations with board approval
Employees must not solicit appointments as executors, administrators or trustees of clients' estates

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff should not engage directly or indirectly in any business activity that competes or conflicts with the market intermediary's interest or those of its clients unless fully disclosed to the clients.

sections.heading: Misuse of Position
sections.title: Prohibition on using market intermediary facilities for personal gain
sections.summary: Directors, management, and staff must not use the market intermediary's name or facilities for personal advantage in political, investment, or retail transactions. They also cannot use their connection to borrow from or become indebted to clients. Additionally, they are prohibited from using their position to obtain preferential treatment in purchasing goods, shares, or securities. This prohibition may lead to dismissal and prosecution.
sections.dtia_summary: Prohibits misuse of market intermediary facilities for personal gain, which could impact cross-border data transfers if client financial data is involved in such transactions.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff must not use the market intermediary's name or facilities for personal advantage in political, investment or retail purchasing transactions
Directors, management and staff must not use their connection with the market intermediary to borrow from or become indebted to clients
Directors, management and staff must not use their position to obtain preferential treatment in purchasing goods, shares and other securities

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff must not use the market intermediary's name or facilities for personal advantage in political, investment or retail purchasing transactions, or in similar types of activities.

sections.heading: Misuse of Information
sections.title: Prohibition on insider trading and misuse of confidential information
sections.summary: Directors, management, and staff are prohibited from trading securities when they possess non-public information that could materially affect the market price. They also cannot influence others to trade or share such information, which is considered insider dealing and is a crime. This section applies to market intermediaries in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: Prohibits insider trading and misuse of confidential information, which could affect data transfers involving client financial information across borders.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff should not deal in securities when in possession of non-public information that could materially affect market prices
Directors, management and staff prohibited from influencing others to deal in securities or communicating insider information

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff should not deal in the securities of any company listed or pending listing on a stock exchange at any time when in possession of information... which would likely bring a material change in the market price of the shares or other securities of the company concerned.

sections.heading: Integrity of Records and Transactions
sections.title: Requirement for accurate and complete financial records
sections.summary: Accounting records and reports must be complete and accurate, with no false entries that obscure transaction nature or mislead authorization limits. All records and computer files must be used only for their intended management purposes to ensure integrity and prevent fraud.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures accurate financial records, which is critical for data transfers involving financial information to prevent misrepresentation or fraud.

requirements.text: Accounting records and reports must be complete and accurate
No false entries or entries that obscure the true nature of transactions or mislead authorization limits
All records and computer files must be accessed and used only for management purposes for which they were originally intended

sections.source_quote: Accounting records and reports must be complete and accurate. Directors, management and staff should never make entries or allow entries to be made for any account, record or document of the market intermediary that are false and would obscure the true nature of the transaction...

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.title: Confidentiality of client information and transactions
sections.summary: Directors, management, and staff must protect client information and transactions from disclosure, except in the proper course of duty or with written consent. They cannot use client information for financial gain. Business information may be shared with third parties only with client consent or as required by law.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires strict confidentiality of client data, which is essential for data transfers to prevent unauthorized access or breaches.

requirements.text: Directors, management and staff must take precautions to protect client information and transactions
No staff, management or director may disclose or use secrets, copyright material, or client correspondence without written consent or proper duty
No staff, management or director may use information obtained for financial gain
Business and financial information about clients may be shared with third parties only with prior written consent or as required by law

sections.source_quote: Confidentiality of relations and dealings between the market intermediary and its clients is paramount... No member of staff, management or director should during, or upon and after termination of employment... divulge or make use of any secrets, copyright material, or any correspondence...

sections.heading: Fair and Equitable Treatment
sections.title: Fair and equitable treatment of clients and staff
sections.summary: All business dealings with clients, staff, and those relying on the market intermediary must be conducted fairly and equitably without influence from friendship or association. Decisions must be arms-length business decisions, avoiding preferential transactions with insiders unless fully documented and authorized by the Board.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures fair treatment in transactions, which is critical for data transfers to prevent bias or preferential handling of client data.

requirements.text: All business dealings with clients, staff, and those relying on the market intermediary must be conducted fairly and equitably
Staff, management and directors must not be influenced by friendship or association in meeting client requirements or recommendations
Decisions must be made on a strictly arms-length business basis
Preferential transactions with insiders must be avoided or, if transacted, must be fully documented and authorized by the Board

sections.source_quote: All business dealing on behalf of the market intermediary with the current potential clients, with other members of staff and with those who may have cause to rely upon the market intermediary should be conducted fairly and equitably.

sections.heading: Insider Loans
sections.title: Prohibition on insider loans and credit facilities
sections.summary: Market intermediaries in Kenya cannot grant unsecured advances to employees or their associates. Loans to officers, significant shareholders, or their associates must be fully secured, approved by the Board, and on terms similar to ordinary clients. Any loans must not be made in a fraudulent or reckless manner.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricts insider loans, which is critical for data transfers involving financial information to prevent conflicts of interest.

requirements.text: Market intermediary shall not grant or permit unsecured advances to employees or their associates
Market intermediary shall not grant unsecured or non-fully secured advances to officers, significant shareholders, or their associates
Loans to directors or other management participants must be approved by the full board of directors
Loans must be made in the normal course of business on terms similar to ordinary clients
Market intermediary must notify the Authority within seven days of approval for loans to directors
Loans must not be made in a fraudulent or reckless manner or otherwise than in compliance with the Act and regulations

sections.source_quote: Directors, management and staff should not use their positions to further their personal interests. A market intermediary shall not in Kenya therefore—
